Data

Data report · Florida DBPR public inspection records · June 2026

Cuisines, ranked by what’s in the kitchen

Pick a cuisine and you think you know what you’re getting. The health scores complicate it. Cafe spots grade cleanest in Central Florida (93.8/100 average, 0% failing). And the freshness halo around sushi? Japanese restaurants average 84.5 — below Mexican’s 88.9. Every cuisine with at least 20 graded restaurants, cleanest to riskiest.

  1. 1 Cafe
    93.8
    78% A 0% D/F 45
  2. 2 Cuban
    91.2
    80% A 10% D/F 30
  3. 3 Pizza
    90.1
    66% A 3% D/F 62
  4. 4 Mexican
    88.9
    63% A 8% D/F 198
  5. 5 Seafood
    87.4
    55% A 11% D/F 93
  6. 6 American
    87.3
    57% A 9% D/F 505
  7. 7 Italian
    87.1
    59% A 13% D/F 135
  8. 8 Japanese
    84.5
    48% A 13% D/F 40
  9. 9 Steak
    83.8
    55% A 15% D/F 20
  10. 10 Chinese
    82.3
    53% A 16% D/F 38

Average score is the mean of every graded restaurant in that cuisine (minimum 20 to qualify). “% D/F” is the share carrying a failing or near-failing grade. A clean café and a dicey steakhouse can sit a full letter apart — the kitchen, not the menu, decides the grade.

More data reports

  • Flagship Loved but Failing Great reviews, failing grades — the rating-vs-safety gap, widest first.

  • Counterintuitive The Disney Paradox The cleanest kitchens in Central Florida are inside Walt Disney World.
  • Trajectories Comebacks & Collapses The biggest health turnarounds and freefalls on record.
  • Enforcement Emergency Orders Where the state took its strongest action.

Source: Florida DBPR public inspection records, as of June 2026. Cuisine labels are third-party; grades are calculated by us from each restaurant’s most recent inspection. Methodology →
